comments_1: Dr. David Hung received a letter dated April 13, 2016 from the Chief Executive Officer of Sanofi.
Non-binding proposal.
Sanofi's proposal was publicly announced.
Pfizer expressed interest in a possible transaction when, and if, Medivation decided to pursue such a transaction.
comments_2: This followed Pfizer's April 20 outreach attempt.

comments_1: Mr. Giordano highlighted Pfizer's complementary oncology focus and reiterated Pfizer's interest in pursuing a negotiated transaction with Medivation when and if Medivation and its Board of Directors decided to pursue such a transaction.
Representatives of J.P. Morgan and Evercore contacted Mr. Giordano to discuss providing Pfizer with confidential information, subject to execution of a confidentiality and standstill agreement.
Confidentiality and standstill agreement.
comments_2: Medivation invited Pfizer to participate in a potential sale process.

comments_1: Medivation's July 5, 2016 press release stated it had entered into confidentiality agreements with several parties, including Sanofi.
On July 5, 2016, Medivation announced that Sanofi had agreed to withdraw its consent solicitation.
comments_2: The same announcement stated that Medivation had entered into a confidentiality agreement with Sanofi.
comments_3: No terminal price or valuation comparison was disclosed in this announcement.

comments_1: Medivation announced it had entered into confidentiality agreements with several parties, including Sanofi, and that Sanofi had agreed to withdraw its consent solicitation.
During a meeting with Dr. Hung, Mr. Read indicated that Pfizer was interested in further exploration of a potential strategic transaction.
comments_2: On the same date, representatives of Pfizer were granted access to a limited virtual data room containing certain non-public information regarding Medivation's business and operations.

comments_1: Medivation requested a written, non-binding preliminary proposal on August 8, 2016.
comments_2: Letter stated a limited number of qualified parties might be invited to further discussions and additional due diligence.

comments_1: Non-binding preliminary proposal.
comments_2: Subject to satisfactory completion of due diligence; no financing condition.
comments_3: Pfizer expected to complete due diligence and sign a definitive transaction agreement in approximately one week.

comments_1: Preliminary proposals were due on this date.
Medivation notified Pfizer it was invited to a subsequent round, together with several other interested parties.
comments_2: Pfizer received access to an expanded virtual data room and a draft Merger Agreement on August 11, 2016.
comments_3: On August 14, 2016, Medivation requested a mark-up of the Merger Agreement by August 18 and a definitive proposal by August 19.

comments_1: Revised written proposal.
comments_2: Centerview discussed the proposal with J.P. Morgan and Evercore.
comments_3: Pfizer was informed later that day to submit its best and final offer on August 20, 2016.

comments_1: Definitive proposals were requested for this date.
On August 19, 2016, representatives of J.P. Morgan and Evercore informed Mr. Giordano that Pfizer should submit its "best and final" offer on Saturday, August 20, 2016.
Following a written request by J.P. Morgan and Evercore, Pfizer's "best and final" proposal was due by the afternoon of August 20, 2016.
comments_2: Pfizer communicated its best and final proposal on that date.

comments_1: Best and final proposal.
comments_2: Submitted following a written request by J.P. Morgan and Evercore to provide Pfizer's best and final proposal by the afternoon of August 20, 2016.

comments_1: Pfizer, Purchaser and Medivation executed the Merger Agreement on the afternoon of Saturday, August 20, 2016.
Pfizer and Medivation issued a joint press release announcing the proposed transaction.
